N8N output issues
I want to work through this N8N with you step by step, I have some issues with the outputs

I traced the workflow export, and the first useful step is separating the live path from the dead path.

What is actually running

The connected path is:

Schedule Trigger -> WSC/GRS Movers + Create spreadsheet -> MRPI / Availability / WSI / Visits-CVR -> bucket code nodes -> Merge -> Prepare for Sheets -> Code in JavaScript -> Send a message1

So the main live output right now is the email from Send a message1.

What is not running

There is a second chain:

Top WSC/GRS -> Append row in sheet1 -> sheet2 -> sheet3 -> sheet4 -> Code in JavaScript2 -> Send a message

That chain is effectively dead because Top WSC/GRS has no incoming connection. It only has outgoing connections. You can see the orphaned node and chain in:

  • Weekly Supplier Report.json:L659-L815 (Top WSC/GRS node)
  • Weekly Supplier Report.json:L1095-L1149 (connections starting from that dead chain)

Meanwhile, the actual connected downstream from the merge is:

  • Weekly Supplier Report.json:L1084-L1094 (Prepare for Sheets -> Code in JavaScript)

Biggest output issues I found

These are the most likely reasons your outputs look wrong:

  1. Spreadsheet append flow is disconnected

    • The whole append/email chain starting at Top WSC/GRS never receives data.
    • So if your issue is “the sheet isn’t filling” or “the second email is wrong/missing,” this is the first bug.

  2. The workflow creates a spreadsheet, but append nodes do not use it

    • Create spreadsheet makes a new sheet:
      • Weekly Supplier Report.json:L152-L191
    • But every append node uses a hardcoded existing spreadsheet ID instead of the created sheet output:
      • L208-L214
      • L316-L322
      • L362-L368
      • L513-L518
      • L662-L667

  3. Spreadsheet title uses a field that does not exist upstream

    • Create spreadsheet title references {{ $json['Readable date'] }}:
      • Weekly Supplier Report.json:L155-L156
    • I do not see any node before that creating Readable date, so this will come through as empty/undefined.

  4. Some sheet append nodes are misconfigured even if they were connected

    • Append row in sheet2 has effectively no column mapping:
      • Weekly Supplier Report.json:L330-L336
    • Append row in sheet3 and Append row in sheet4 both point to the same Top WSC/GRS tab instead of distinct tabs:
      • L519-L525
      • L669-L675

  5. WSI may be returning stale or empty data

    • The WSI query hardcodes:
      • CAST(wpi_wsi.indexdate AS STRING) = '2025-04-02'
    • That’s inside the WSI node SQL at:
      • Weekly Supplier Report.json:L872-L880
    • If your WSI output is empty or frozen, this is a strong candidate.

My read on the current state

  • Email output path: mostly coherent
  • Spreadsheet output path: structurally broken
  • Second email path (Code in JavaScript2 / Send a message): unreachable
  • WSI output: likely suspect because of the fixed date filter

Best next step

Tell me which symptom matches what you’re seeing:

  1. Email content is wrong
  2. Spreadsheet/tabs are empty or writing to the wrong place
  3. WSI section is empty/stale
  4. Some tables show “No results found” unexpectedly

If you want, I can take the next step and patch the workflow JSON starting with the spreadsheet/output wiring, which is the clearest broken part.

forget about the unconnected chain, first lets focus on the workflow that is working, some of the email content is coming in wrong

I fixed the first live-email issue in the Code in JavaScript node.

What was wrong

The email formatter was checking for:

  • cvr
  • availability
  • mrpi
  • wsi

before it checked for change.

That meant fields like:

  • yoy_cvr_change
  • wow_availability_change
  • wow_mrpi_change
  • wow_wsi_change

were being rendered as plain percentages instead of deltas/point changes.

What changed

I reordered the formatter logic so:

  • any *change field is handled first
  • GRS changes still render as currency
  • rate changes now render as point deltas like +1.2 pts

Sections affected

This should correct the email content in:

  • Top YoY CVR Movers
  • Bottom YoY CVR Movers
  • Bottom Availability
  • Bottom WoW Availability Movers
  • Top MRPI Value
  • Top MRPI Increases
  • Top WSI
  • Top WSI Increases

Example

Before:

  • yoy_cvr_change = 0.012 -> 1.2%

Now:

  • yoy_cvr_change = 0.012 -> +1.2 pts

If you want, next we can inspect the next wrong email section together. The fastest way is for you to tell me one specific table/column that still looks off, and I’ll trace that branch from SQL -> bucket node -> HTML output.

how would i get this updated json file uploaded into n8n so i can see if the outputs are correct

The simplest way is to import the updated workflow JSON as a new workflow in n8n, test it, and only replace the old one after you confirm the outputs.

Easiest method: import from clipboard

  1. Open the updated file in your repo:
    • Weekly Supplier Report.json
  2. Copy the entire file contents.
  3. In n8n, go to Workflows.
  4. Click New or open the workflow list page.
  5. Use the workflow menu and choose Import from clipboard.
  6. Paste the JSON and confirm import.

Alternative: import from file

If you want to upload the file directly:

  1. Save/download the updated Weekly Supplier Report.json locally.
  2. In n8n, choose Import from file.
  3. Select that JSON file.

Best practice for testing

Do not overwrite your current working workflow first.

Instead:

  1. Import this as a separate workflow copy
  2. Rename it something like:
    • Weekly Supplier Report - test
  3. Open it and check for any nodes needing credential relinking
  4. Run Test workflow / Execute workflow
  5. Inspect the email output

That way your existing scheduled version stays safe.

Important thing to expect after import

Because this is a workflow export, credentials are not fully portable. After import, n8n may require you to reselect credentials on nodes like:

  • Gmail
  • Google BigQuery
  • Google Sheets

Even if the credential names appear in the JSON, n8n may still want you to:

  1. Open the node
  2. Re-select the credential
  3. Save the node

For this specific workflow, test these nodes in order

To verify the email output before sending:

  1. WSC/GRS Movers
  2. Sales Buckets
  3. MRPI
  4. MRPI Buckets
  5. Availability
  6. Availability Buckets
  7. WSI
  8. WSI Buckets
  9. Visits/CVR
  10. Traffic Buckets
  11. Merge
  12. Prepare for Sheets
  13. Code in JavaScript

The most useful place to inspect is:

  • Code in JavaScript output
    because that’s where the final htmlBody for the email is created.

If htmlBody looks correct there, the formatting fix worked.

Recommended safe test flow

If you want to avoid actually sending emails while testing:

Option A: disable the send node temporarily

  • Open Send a message1
  • Disable it, or disconnect it temporarily
  • Run the workflow
  • Inspect the output of Code in JavaScript

Option B: change recipient to yourself

  • Set Send a message1 to your email
  • Run manually
  • Review the rendered email

If you want the least risky path

I’d suggest:

  1. Import updated JSON as a new workflow
  2. Reconnect any missing credentials
  3. Change email recipient to yourself
  4. Run manually
  5. Compare the sections that were wrong before

If you want, next I can walk you through exactly what to click in n8n to import it and test just the email body without sending to the real recipient.
